name: Miraflores de la Sierra, Canencia, Lozoya, Rascafría – circular route
name_original: Miraflores de la Sierra, Canencia, Lozoya, Rascafría – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling around Miraflores de la Sierra offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, known for its challenging mountain passes and scenic landscapes. The region features wooded areas, meadows, and forests of ash, holm oak, pine, and oak trees. Cyclists encounter significant climbs, notably the La Morcuera and Canencia passes, which provide panoramic mountain views and generally good road surfaces. The area's varied topography includes both demanding ascents and numerous kilometers of forest tracks. Best road cycling routes around Miraflores de la Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Miraflores de la Sierra, Canencia, Lozoya, Rascafría – circular route, a difficult 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This route navigates through multiple mountain passes, offering sustained climbs and descents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Morcuera Pass loop from Ctra. M611 - Los Colladillos, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.9 km) path. This route features the classic climb to La Morcuera Pass, known for its panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. Local road cyclists also love the El Villar Reservoir – Canencia Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra, a 63.7 miles (102.6 km) trail leading through the El Villar Reservoir area and over the Canencia Pass, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Miraflores de la Sierra is defined by challenging mountain passes, diverse forests, and open meadows. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate climbs to difficult, long-distance routes. The routes in Miraflores de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 12,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Miraflores de la Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around Miraflores de la Sierra, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 12,000 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra, located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, offers diverse terrain. You can expect challenging mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass&lt;/a&gt;, along with scenic wooded landscapes, meadows, and forests of ash, holm oak, pine, and oak trees. The area is known for its significant climbs and panoramic mountain views.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for easier rides?
A: Yes, while the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are also routes suitable for beginners. Out of the 73 available routes, 7 are classified as easy. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39562476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto del Real Cycling Area – Lisboa Gastrobar loop from Soto del Real&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate path with less elevation gain.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling climbs in the area?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra is a prime location for challenging climbs, often referred to as a 'mecca of cycling.' The most notable passes are &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977037072/miraflores-de-la-sierra-canencia-lozoya-rascafria-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miraflores de la Sierra, Canencia, Lozoya, Rascafría – circular route&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir – Canencia Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and are popular among experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977037072/miraflores-de-la-sierra-canencia-lozoya-rascafria-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miraflores de la Sierra, Canencia, Lozoya, Rascafría – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir – Canencia Pass loop from Miraflores de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: Beyond the stunning mountain views from passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia&lt;/a&gt;, you can encounter natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass by reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/434252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While summer can be warm, the higher elevations of the mountain passes offer cooler air. Winter cycling is possible, but conditions can be more challenging, especially at higher altitudes, due to colder temperatures and potential snow.

Q: Is public transport available to Miraflores de la Sierra for cyclists?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra is conveniently located less than 50 km from Madrid, making it accessible. Public transport options, such as buses, connect the town to Madrid. It's advisable to check with the specific bus operator regarding their policy on carrying bicycles, as this can vary.

Q: Are there places to park my car if I drive to Miraflores de la Sierra for cycling?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra is a popular starting point for cyclists, and as such, you can typically find parking within the town. Many cyclists park in or around the town center before embarking on their rides into the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the challenge and panoramic views offered by the mountain passes, and the generally good road surfaces. The diverse landscapes, from wooded areas to open meadows, also contribute to a highly enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on established roads within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. However, it's always recommended to respect park regulations, stay on marked routes, and be aware of any specific restrictions that might apply to certain areas or during particular seasons.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along the road cycling routes in Miraflores de la Sierra?
A: Miraflores de la Sierra itself is a charming town with various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. While some routes might pass through smaller villages with amenities, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote sections of your journey.

It is a huge viaduct located in the heart of the Lozoya valley. One of the most important engineering works in the entire Sierra Norte de Madrid. It has a length of 240 meters.
The viewpoint has incredible views, not only of the reservoir but also of Guadarrama and Peñalara in the background. A quiet place, full of peace, and a good place to rest.

Rascafría is a beautiful village in the heart of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range. Located in the Lozoya Valley, it boasts an ideal location for cycling between the Morcuera and Cotos mountain passes.

Its main monument is the Monastery of Santa María del Paular, nestled in a privileged setting blanketed with pine and oak forests. As its name suggests, it is one of the coldest villages in the Madrid region.
